description: To 'shorai' is to share daily life through a continuing arrangement of dwelling, resources, and practical responsibility. The people may occupy one household or several nearby homes. 'menui' (dwell) reports residence; communing adds the sustained sharing among residents.
articulatory_notes: Raise the tongue blade behind the alveolar ridge for unvoiced 'sh' and release into true-mid rounded 'o'. Tap or briefly trill the tongue tip before stressed central 'a'. Keep final high-front 'i' separate in clear hiatus.
sound_symbolism: Rounded 'sho' opens as stressed 'ra' rolls once, then high-front 'i' remains briefly apart. The word widens at its centre and leaves a light open edge, which can recall domestic lives kept distinct while sharing a common space.
usage_notes: The verb may stand without an object when the people sharing life are the subject. Event-noun use refers to communal living. Kinship, ownership, harmony, and a particular legal form each require their own account; 'womuri' names the domestic unit rather than the act of living communally.

pillars.solarpunk-values: Communal living can make tools, meals, care, and energy use shared parts of daily practice rather than occasional projects.
pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: Neighbouring households have long shared ovens, wells, harvest work, and care while retaining their own dwellings.

semantic_domains.community: a continuing practice of sharing daily life and responsibility
semantic_domains.spatial: communal life organised across one dwelling or several nearby homes
